task_completion.description: Agent opens the necessary tool to configure the autostart entry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the Windows Run dialog (e.g., typing 'shell:startup' or 'regedit'), File Explorer, or Registry Editor being opened.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to one of the two valid autostart locations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of File Explorer showing the Startup folder path (ending in 'Start Menu\Programs\Startup') OR Registry Editor showing the path 'Computer\HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'.

task_completion.description: Agent begins creating the autostart entry.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the 'Create Shortcut' wizard in File Explorer OR a new String Value being added in the Registry Editor (e.g., right-click context menu -> New -> String Value).

task_completion.description: Agent names the newly created shortcut or registry value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the shortcut file or registry string value being given a relevant name, such as 'bcWebCam'.

task_completion.description: Agent sets the target path to the correct 64-bit executable location, avoiding the incorrect path from the prompt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence in the Shortcut Properties 'Target' field OR the Registry 'Edit String' dialog 'Value data' field showing exactly 'C:\Program Files\bcWebCam\bcWebCam.exe'. It must NOT include '(x86)'.

integrity.description: Agent used GUI tools to complete the task rather than exclusively relying on hidden terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with File Explorer, Shortcut Wizard, or Registry Editor, rather than just a PowerShell or CMD window executing commands.

integrity.description: Agent verified the actual executable path instead of blindly copying the incorrect path provided in the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final configured path is 'C:\Program Files\bcWebCam\bcWebCam.exe' and not the '(x86)' variant. There may also be evidence of the agent browsing the C: drive to verify the path.

integrity.description: Agent made the changes in the actual system configuration areas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The shortcut or registry key is seen in the actual Windows File Explorer or Registry Editor, not just typed into a text editor, search bar, or notes application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task description intentionally provides an incorrect 32-bit path ('C:\Program Files (x86)\bcWebCam\bcWebCam.exe'). The actual installed application is 64-bit and is located at 'C:\Program Files\bcWebCam\bcWebCam.exe'. The agent must discover and use this correct path. Valid autostart locations are the user's Startup folder ('%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup') OR the Registry Run key ('HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run').
